topic_title: What an API is — a door into another system; request and response
verdict: PASS — intent fully covered. 4-node LR pipeline shows the complete request-response round trip with numbered edge labels (1. POST request → 2. forwards → 3. returns → 4. back to client). Client (blue), API Endpoint (indigo), Anthropic Server (yellow), JSON Response (green) — color tiers semantically appropriate. 'runs Claude internally' on Server node correctly names the internal system without teaching it. Numbered edges give beginners explicit reading order. Scope clean to 12.9 concepts.

node_count.note: YOUR Python Code → API Endpoint → Anthropic Server → JSON Response. Cycle via edge 4 back to client. Minimal and complete.

label_clarity.note: All labels plain-language: 'Your Python Code / the client', 'API Endpoint / URL + method + headers + body', 'Anthropic Server / runs Claude internally', 'JSON Response / status code + body'. Subtitles reinforce main labels.

intent_coverage.intent: Client-server request-response cycle: Your Python code (client) → API endpoint → Server → Response back to client.
intent_coverage.note: All 4 stages of the round trip present and sequenced. Numbered steps make the reading order explicit.

scope_discipline.note: No JSON parsing nodes (12.12), no Anthropic library code nodes (12.11), no rate limit/API key nodes beyond what the corpus §7 covers conceptually. Clean.
